latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

Share SEDOL:
BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group builds and supplies precision machinery and components for industrial customers, offering custom solutions that keep factories, power plants and infrastructure projects running smoothly.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Their equipment powers critical manufacturing lines and energy facilities, giving them a niche in sectors that need reliable, high‑quality hardware. The firm’s long‑term contracts with large OEMs give it a steady revenue base and a foothold in markets that are hard to enter.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase, trimming costs, returning capital to shareholders, and raising fresh funds. It is trading at a very low price, reflecting a distressed valuation that has been driven down by recent cash‑burn and weak earnings.

rns_narrative: The latest updates focus on capital management: the firm has just released a new net‑asset‑value factsheet, announced a return of capital to shareholders, and reported its AGM results. These moves signal an attempt to shore up shareholder value.
Separately, the company is updating its portfolio and holdings, giving investors a clearer view of what assets drive its book value. This transparency may help calm concerns about hidden risk.
Overall, the narrative is one of a company trying to stabilize its finances and keep investors informed, rather than announcing new growth initiatives.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but cautious – the community is waiting for a clear signal before reacting.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts praise the low NAV and the potential for a big discount to intrinsic value, suggesting a contrarian opportunity if the company can turn its finances around.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of new product launches and the company’s heavy cash‑burn as reasons to stay away, warning that the firm may struggle to sustain operations without fresh revenue.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat over the past year; no new enthusiasm has emerged and no fresh negative headlines have appeared.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is essentially dead, meaning the market is not tracking this stock. A surprise catalyst could therefore generate a sharp reaction.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming half‑year report could reveal a turnaround in cash flow or a new partnership that boosts demand for its machinery.
If the company announces a dividend or a further return of capital, it would signal confidence and could lift the price.
A sudden increase in trading volume that breaks the current 52‑week low support level would be a strong technical sign that a bounce is starting.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis are: (1) a positive earnings surprise of at least 10 % over the prior quarter, (2) a volume spike exceeding 150 % of the 20‑day average, and (3) a price break above the 20‑day moving average.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery – the crash is likely finished, but the market has yet to react to any new positive information.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the lack of a clear contrarian signal and the absence of a catalyst keep the outlook uncertain.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company successfully returns capital and shows improved cash flow, the price could rebound to the mid‑range of its 52‑week swing, delivering roughly a 200 % upside. The mean‑reversion thesis relies on the fact that the market has already punished the stock for a long time, so a modest improvement could trigger a large rally.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm still faces high cash burn and no new growth initiatives. If earnings continue to miss expectations, the price could fall another 30 % before stabilising, leaving a small upside window.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30 % of the current price. Potential gain: 200 % if the price climbs to the mid‑range of its 52‑week swing. Risk‑reward ratio ≈ 1 : 6.7, but the upside is highly dependent on a positive earnings announcement.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the earnings report shows a 10 %+ improvement and volume spikes. Size the position to risk no more than 5 % of the portfolio per trade. Place a stop 10 % below the entry price to protect against a further decline.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a dividend declaration or a capital‑return announcement. If the price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with volume above 120 % of the 20‑day average, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has surfaced, so the default stance remains neutral. If the company were to file for insolvency or be delisted, the position would be closed immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The setup is neutral – the company is quietly working on financial stabilisation, and a bounce would need a clear earnings turnaround or capital‑return move to materialise.
